name: Prisoner #1424 | Crunchy
description:
A ferocious looking crocodile with a highly disagreeable personality. He's looking for a fight.

Perhaps within the prison walls, fighting is the only way he can relieve his boredom. So, is it better to just put up with it, or stand up to him? You need to make a choice.

Crunchy had a miserable childhood. His mother was a prostitute, and he hid not know who his father was. His stepfather used his mother's hard-earned money to pay off his debts, and after drinking, he would drag young Crunchy into a small dark room and beat him.

Crunchy endured this life of misery for 10 years, until he could not take it any longer. When he was 12 years old, he bit clean through his stepfather's neck, thus ending his childhood's story.

After several years in a juvenile detention facility, Crunchy was finally released. He went home to look for his mother, but she was nowhere to be found. Feeling abandoned by fate, he lost purpose in life and wound up on the streets, relying on charities to get by. That was until one day, when he meet Bonnie.

She was also a crocodilian, but unlike Crunchy, she was not a large saltwater crocodile, she was not a large saltwater crocodile, but a petite alligator. Bonnie's childhood was not much easier than Crunchy's, but she always managed to keep smiling and stay optimistic. She told Crunchy that since fate had abandoned them, they should teach fate a lesson.

Their first robbery was a small restaurant. Crunchy was so nervous that he could not speak, but luckily Bonnie was there. She jumped up onto a table and shouted: "Ladies and Gentlemen, this is a robbery! If anyone misbehaves, my big friend here will bite your head off!" As Crunchy looked at Bonnie on the table, the corners of his mouth curled into a smile, something that had not happened for many years. He thought how she was so violent but also so beautiful. Before long, the "Croc Duo" swept through all of the major western cities. Hotels, supermarkets, gas stations...

There was no place they were not willing to target. According to the media, the couple did not always commit robberies for money, but sometimes just for fun.

However, Bonnie's appetite was no longer satisfied by petty robberies, and she told Crunchy that she wanted to rob a bank: "It'll be so much fun, honey!" Of course, the smitten Crunchy could not refuse. So on a hot, sunny afternoon, they burst into a small bank on the outskirts of town with guns at the ready. But much to Crunchy's surprise, the bank was completely empty-not a customer or clerk in sight. It turned out that federal agents had tracked them down, and as the sirens wailed, they knew the game was over.

Bonnie laughed gleefully, as if she was the final winner. She rushed out of the bank, and Crunchy followed after. Just then, two gunshots rang out, and Crunchy fell to the ground.

Despite being shot in the head, Crunchy miraculously survived. When he woke up, he asked if Bonnie was alive or dead, but the police all played dumb. They claimed that they had never seen any girl and that he was the only crocodile robber! To him, this was an absurd lie; he would not fall for it. They must have captured Bonnie and locked her up in secret. "That must be it. It must be..." he told himself.

Art by【 Fug@9 】

From Back to the Dawn
creator: Linussy
